focus_points.label: Late nights — temporary push or default?
focus_points.reason: Notes say Lin has worked late for about three weeks. Could be a short runway to finish two major features ahead of schedule, or it might be turning into the new normal—worth clarifying what’s driving the load now that launches are done.

focus_points.label: What Lin is saying yes to now
focus_points.reason: Whether the next cycle’s priorities are still the same kind of push work, or if day-to-day firefighting is creeping in after the launches. This helps you understand what she’s optimizing for and whether the sequence of bets is sustainable.

focus_points.label: What only Lin should own vs delegate
focus_points.reason: Whether sustained late nights are hiding a distribution or autonomy problem. A head-of-product at this level should be able to move outcomes through others—so you’ll want to get specific about what’s still too central to her, versus what could be owned by leads or partners.

focus_points.label: What Lin wants more of from you
focus_points.reason: How she handles concern can make it easy for you to miss what she’s carrying. Use the bi-weekly format to ask directly for the concrete support she wants from you (air cover, decisions, or time), so she doesn’t have to brush things off to keep momentum.
